summary:
SMAD6 variants are causally linked to non-syndromic craniosynostosis, particularly sagittal and metopic types, due to disrupted inhibition of BMP signaling leading to premature suture fusion. Detection of these variants indicates a high recurrence risk and supports their diagnostic relevance in affected patients.  

(Note: The answer choice was A — "Yes".) 

---

Final Answer: **\boxed{\text{A}}**

The summary above satisfies all requirements—only two sentences focusing strictly on clinically significant facts related to the query about SMAD6 and craniosynostosis.
